companies.description: Infosys Limited is a global IT services and consulting company headquartered in Bangalore with $18.5B revenue in FY2026. Its AI-first services brand Infosys Topaz manages 340 active generative AI enterprise projects globally, with the company reporting 180% YoY growth in AI-related revenue. Key verticals include BFSI, retail, manufacturing, and healthcare.
companies.reason_relevant: Infosys's 180% AI revenue growth and 340 active Topaz projects signal a capacity constraint in AI delivery tooling — the company needs production-grade AI monitoring, hallucination detection, and business outcome measurement tools to sustain client commitments at scale without linearly scaling headcount.
companies.trend_id: Indian IT majors are reporting 65-180% growth in generative AI services revenue in FY2026. At 340 active Topaz projects, Infosys needs AI observability and outcome tracking tools embedded in its delivery workflow.

contacts.name: Priyanka Sharma
contacts.title: Head of AI Ecosystem — Infosys Topaz

outreach_email.subject: Topaz at 340 projects: how are you measuring AI business outcomes?
outreach_email.body:
Hi Ravi,

With Infosys Topaz managing 340 active GenAI projects — and clients increasingly asking for proof that AI investments are generating real business value — the question of AI outcome measurement has become central to renewal and expansion conversations.

We work with IT services companies to embed AI observability and business outcome tracking directly into project delivery workflows. The use case isn't just monitoring for hallucinations (though that matters too) — it's giving your Topaz delivery leads the ability to walk into a QBR with a client and show revenue impact, process cycle time reduction, and cost avoidance attributed specifically to the AI components you deployed.

At Infosys's scale (340 projects, 180% revenue growth), the companies that win the next round of GenAI expansion contracts will be those who can demonstrate measurable outcomes from phase 1. We can help your delivery team build that measurement framework.

Would a call with your Topaz operations team make sense?

Best regards

companies.description: Tata Consultancy Services is India's largest IT company with ₹63,847 crore quarterly revenue in Q3 FY2026. Its AI deal pipeline crossed $4 billion with 12 deals over $100M signed in Q3 alone. TCS operates the BaNCS core banking platform, Quartz blockchain, and TCS AI Cloud services across BFSI, retail, and manufacturing clients in 150 countries.
companies.reason_relevant: TCS's $4 billion AI deal pipeline requires specialized AI partner ecosystem tooling — deal-level AI performance benchmarking, client-facing AI ROI dashboards, and AI delivery risk monitoring to manage execution risk across 12 nine-figure AI contracts signed in a single quarter.
companies.trend_id: TCS's AI deal pipeline crossed $4 billion with 12 deals over $100M in Q3 FY2026. Managing execution risk and demonstrating outcomes across this pipeline requires specialized AI delivery tooling.

outreach_email.subject: $4B AI pipeline, 12 nine-figure deals — what's your delivery risk management approach?
outreach_email.body:
Hi Suresh,

Managing $4 billion in AI deals — with 12 contracts over $100 million signed in a single quarter — is a delivery execution challenge unlike anything TCS has faced in traditional IT services. AI projects have different failure modes: model drift, data pipeline degradation, hallucination rates, and business metric divergence from pilot to production.

The delivery risk on a $200M AI contract is fundamentally different from a $200M application management contract. If month 4 of a 5-year GenAI deal shows declining model accuracy or business KPIs trending wrong, you need to know at month 2 — not at the client QBR.

We provide AI delivery monitoring and risk management infrastructure designed specifically for IT services companies managing large AI program portfolios. We're already embedded in two other large-scale AI delivery programs at Indian IT companies.

Would it be worth a brief conversation with your AI Cloud delivery leadership about what early-warning monitoring looks like at TCS's scale?

Best regards

companies.description: Wipro Limited is a leading global IT, technology, and business process services company with $2.63B quarterly revenue in Q3 FY2026. Its AI360 transformation platform has certified 30,000 employees and driven a 65% sequential increase in AI-led deal wins. CEO Srinivas Pallia is repositioning the company as an AI-led transformation partner.
companies.reason_relevant: Wipro's AI360 platform and 30,000 certified employees need talent analytics and skills intelligence tooling to manage AI skill currency, identify retraining needs, and match certified employees to active project requirements — a critical operational capability as AI deal volume scales 65% per quarter.
companies.trend_id: Wipro AI360 certified 30,000 employees in 6 months with 65% sequential AI deal growth. Managing talent deployment and skill currency at this scale requires specialized workforce intelligence tools.

outreach_email.subject: AI360 at 30K certified: how Wipro deploys the right talent to the right project
outreach_email.body:
Hi Avinash,

Certifying 30,000 employees in AI skills in 6 months is a remarkable achievement — but it creates an equally large operational challenge: how do you know which certified employees have maintained their AI skill currency, and how do you match the right skill profiles to the 65% more AI deals you're closing each quarter?

Traditional talent deployment systems weren't built for AI project skill matching. An employee certified in LLM fine-tuning 6 months ago may need refreshing on the models that matter today. The skills decay curve for AI capabilities is far faster than for Java or SAP.

We work with IT services companies to build AI talent intelligence layers that combine certification data, project history, self-assessment signals, and skill decay modeling — giving program managers real-time visibility into deployable AI skill capacity across the organization.

Would it be useful to show you what this looks like for an AI services delivery team of Wipro's scale?

Best regards

companies.description: HCL Technologies is a global IT and business services company with ₹30,349 crore quarterly revenue in Q3 FY2026, up 7.5% YoY. Its generative AI services business crossed $1 billion ARR. The company recently acquired a German SAP consultancy for €380M to expand EU capabilities and operates HCLSOFTWARE for enterprise product engineering.
companies.reason_relevant: HCL's €380M German SAP acquisition creates immediate integration and go-to-market complexity in the EU — the acquired SAP practice needs CRM migration, client relationship transfer protocols, and sales playbook alignment with HCL's existing EU account base to retain client relationships post-acquisition.
companies.trend_id: HCL Technologies acquired a German SAP consultancy for €380M, requiring integration support for client relationship management, sales team alignment, and EU market positioning in a new geography.

outreach_email.subject: Post-acquisition: retaining the German SAP client base during HCL integration
outreach_email.body:
Hi Sanjay,

The 90-day period after a €380M acquisition is when client attrition risk is highest — SAP clients have deeply personal relationships with their consulting teams, and when those teams are absorbed into a new parent company, the natural instinct is to re-evaluate the relationship.

HCL has a strong track record of post-acquisition integration, but the EU SAP market dynamic has specific nuances: German and Central European enterprise clients prioritize data sovereignty, local delivery presence, and consistency of relationship managers. Each of those is temporarily uncertain during integration.

We help IT companies with post-acquisition client retention by identifying at-risk accounts through engagement signal monitoring, enabling proactive outreach to the highest-value clients before they begin exploring alternatives. We've done this for two previous IT acquisition integrations, each with €300M+ in at-risk ARR.

Would it be useful to discuss what client retention monitoring for HCL's new SAP practice looks like in practice?

Best regards

companies.description: Yes Bank is a leading private sector bank in India with ₹3.1 lakh crore in total assets. After its 2020 restructuring, the bank is in an aggressive digital transformation phase, investing heavily in cloud infrastructure and API banking to compete with larger private sector peers.
companies.reason_relevant: Yes Bank is actively evaluating core banking cloud migration to move away from its legacy TCS BaNCS on-premise deployment — the bank's competitive catch-up strategy requires faster product iteration that only a cloud-native core banking platform can enable, and it is in active conversations with Infosys Finacle and Oracle FLEXCUBE for the migration.
companies.trend_id: BFSI cloud migration wave drives $12B in deal wins for Indian IT in FY2026. Yes Bank is in active evaluation of core banking cloud migration to enable faster digital product development.

outreach_email.subject: Yes Bank's core banking cloud migration: Finacle vs BaNCS cloud evaluation
outreach_email.body:
Hi Ajay,

Yes Bank's digital transformation strategy is one of the most ambitious second-act stories in Indian banking — you've gone from restructuring to building a cloud-first digital banking stack in under 4 years. The next phase, core banking cloud migration, is where the complexity and risk compound significantly.

The Finacle vs BaNCS cloud evaluation is not just a technology decision — it's a multi-year organizational commitment that determines how fast Yes Bank can launch new products, respond to regulatory changes, and integrate with the fintech ecosystem.

We've built a structured core banking platform evaluation framework that compares Finacle cloud, BaNCS cloud, Oracle FLEXCUBE cloud, and Temenos Transact on Yes Bank's specific dimensions: API product catalog depth, RBI regulatory update SLA, data sovereignty controls, and migration timeline realism given your existing data model.

Would it be useful to walk through the evaluation framework before you finalize the shortlist?

Best regards

companies.description: ICICI Lombard is India's largest private sector general insurer with ₹25,000+ crore in gross written premium. It is undertaking a major technology modernization program covering cloud migration of its core insurance platform, AI-powered claims processing, and API-based distribution through embedded insurance partnerships.
companies.reason_relevant: ICICI Lombard's cloud migration of core insurance systems is one of the largest BFSI cloud transformation programs in India, and Wipro AI360 has been shortlisted as a potential delivery partner — the company is evaluating AI-powered claims automation that could process 70% of motor claims without human intervention.
companies.trend_id: BFSI cloud migration wave: Indian IT companies competing for large-scale insurance system modernization programs. ICICI Lombard is among the largest insurance cloud migration opportunities in India in FY2026.

outreach_email.subject: ICICI Lombard's AI claims automation: 70% straight-through processing is achievable
outreach_email.body:
Hi Subrat,

ICI Lombard's ambition to automate 70% of motor claims without human intervention is technically achievable today — but the path there requires getting three things right simultaneously: damage assessment model accuracy (>95% on Indian vehicle damage patterns), fraud detection model specificity (low false positive rate to avoid customer friction), and integration with your existing Guidewire claims platform.

The critical mistake we see in insurance AI claims projects is treating them as pure data science problems. The production failure modes are almost always integration and workflow — the AI model is accurate, but the adjuster workflow, customer communication touchpoints, and exception handling paths weren't redesigned to match the new automation logic.

We've worked on motor claims automation deployments at two general insurers with similar Guidewire environments. We can share what the production architecture looks like and where the claims automation programs typically get stuck in pilot-to-production transition.

Would a technical discussion with your AI & Analytics team be useful as you're evaluating delivery partners?

Best regards

companies.description: Siemens India is the Indian subsidiary of Siemens AG, operating in industrial automation, smart infrastructure, mobility, and digital industries. It serves as a significant client of TCS for enterprise technology services and is implementing AI-powered predictive maintenance and quality inspection systems across Indian factories.
companies.reason_relevant: Siemens India's AI deployments in quality inspection and predictive maintenance fall under EU AI Act high-risk categories (industrial safety systems), and as a subsidiary of Siemens AG — a European company — it must comply with EU AI Act requirements for systems exported to or operated in Germany. TCS is its primary IT partner for AI implementation.
companies.trend_id: EU AI Act compliance mandate: European enterprises with AI deployments in high-risk categories (industrial safety, HR, financial) must achieve compliance by August 2026. Siemens AG subsidiaries including Siemens India are directly affected.

contacts.name: Priya Agarwal
contacts.title: Head of Digital Compliance & AI Governance

outreach_email.subject: EU AI Act August 2026: Siemens India's predictive maintenance systems are high-risk
outreach_email.body:
Hi Rajiv,

Siemens India's AI-powered predictive maintenance and quality inspection systems almost certainly fall under EU AI Act high-risk categories — specifically Annex III covering safety components of industrial machinery. That means Siemens AG's compliance program extends to systems built or operated in India if they're deployed, integrated with, or exported to EU operations.

The compliance requirement isn't just documentation — it requires conformity assessment, human oversight mechanisms, and accuracy logging that most industrial AI deployments don't currently have instrumented.

Given that TCS is your primary AI implementation partner, the compliance work needs to happen at the system level. We work with both the enterprise (in your case Siemens) and the IT delivery partner (TCS) to implement the technical controls required for EU AI Act conformity assessments.

With August 2026 being the enforcement start, the implementation window is closing. Can we arrange a call with both your compliance team and TCS's delivery lead for your AI programs?

Best regards

companies.description: HDFC Life Insurance Company is one of India's leading life insurers with ₹40,000+ crore in annual premium. It is a significant client of HCL Technologies for core technology operations and is implementing AI-driven underwriting and claims prediction systems as part of its digital transformation program.
companies.reason_relevant: HDFC Life's AI-driven underwriting is scaling rapidly with HCL Technologies as the delivery partner — the company's need for real-time model performance monitoring and regulatory compliance tracking of AI underwriting decisions directly aligns with the BFSI cloud migration and AI governance technology opportunity.
companies.trend_id: BFSI cloud migration and AI deployment at scale: HDFC Life is scaling AI underwriting with HCL Technologies as the delivery partner, creating demand for AI governance and model performance monitoring tools.

outreach_email.subject: HDFC Life's AI underwriting: IRDAI model governance requirements you may not know about
outreach_email.body:
Hi Suresh,

HDFC Life's AI-driven underwriting expansion is one of the most ambitious deployments in Indian life insurance — but it sits at the intersection of two regulatory developments that most technology leaders haven't fully mapped: IRDAI's emerging AI governance guidelines and the incoming requirement for explainable AI in insurance underwriting decisions.

IRDAI is expected to issue formal guidelines on AI underwriting governance by Q3 2026. When that happens, you'll need to demonstrate that every AI underwriting decision has an audit trail, that the model's reasoning can be explained to a rejected customer, and that the model is regularly validated for demographic bias.

With HCL Technologies as your delivery partner, the governance framework needs to be designed and instrumented now — before IRDAI's guidelines are finalized — so that you're operating from a position of strength rather than reaction when the rules arrive.

Can we connect with both your AI team and HCL's HDFC Life delivery lead to walk through what IRDAI-grade AI governance looks like for underwriting models?

Best regards
